question: can you check the chapter contradictions you will get the icici lombard aquisition date contradiction as feb 24 and feb 29 check where in the document feb 24 is mentioned and where feb 29 is mentioned and whether that contradiction is correct or not
final_answer:
Excellent! Both lookups returned very detailed, grounded results. Here is the full breakdown:

---

## 🔍 ICICI Lombard Acquisition Date — Contradiction Analysis

### ✅ Is There a Real Contradiction?

**Yes — but it is isolated to a single data entry error on Page 332**, and here's the complete picture:

---

### 📌 Where "February 24, 2024" Appears

| Location | Context |
|---|---|
| **Page 332** — Statutory Subsidiary Table | Column: *"The date since when subsidiary was acquired"* → recorded as **February 24, 2024** for ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company Limited (51.27% shareholding) |
| **Page 83** | An Audit Committee meeting date — **completely unrelated** to ICICI Lombard |

> So "February 24" appears in the ICICI Lombard context **only once** — in the subsidiary table on Page 332.

---

### 📌 Where "February 29, 2024" Appears

**February 29, 2024** is the overwhelmingly dominant date, cited **16 times** across **13 pages** (pp. 72, 141, 142, 227, 279, 283, 284, 300, 311, 312, 318, 328, 330, 332), always with the same consistent language:

> *"ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company Limited ceased to be an associate and became a subsidiary of the Bank with effect from **February 29, 2024**."*

Critically, **the footnote on the very same Page 332** where "February 24" appears in the table also states:
> *"ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company Limited ceased to be an associate and became a subsidiary of the Bank w.e.f. **February 29, 2024**."*

---

### ⚖️ Verdict

| | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Correct Date** | ✅ **February 29, 2024** — confirmed by 16 consistent references across the document |
| **Incorrect Date** | ❌ **February 24, 2024** — appears only once, in the subsidiary table on Page 332 |
| **Nature of Contradiction** | A **data entry error** in the table on Page 332 — the correct date even appears in the footnote on that same page |
| **Is it a real contradiction?** | **Yes**, but it is a clear **typo/clerical error** (24 vs. 29), not a genuine dispute about the event |
| **Confidence** | Very high — February 29 is a leap day in 2024, making it a legally and factually precise date, and it is corroborated across the entire document |

**In short:** The contradiction is real but one-sided — **"February 24"** in the Page 332 table is almost certainly a clerical/typographical error. The correct and universally supported acquisition date is **February 29, 2024**.
